videos.title: How Steve Jobs became Steve Jobs | The Vergecast
videos.description: Long before Steve Jobs was the unstoppable force of nature atop Apple, shipping hit product after hit product, he was practically run out of the company after a series of bad product and management decisions. But as Geoffrey Cain argues in his new book, Steve Jobs in Exile: The Untold Story of NeXT and the Remaking of an American Visionary, the 12 years Jobs spent outside of Apple turned him into the leader the world came to know. Cain joins the show to talk about Jobs' experiences at NeXT and P

videos.title: Apple WWDC 2026 keynote in 25 minutes
videos.description:
Apple just wrapped WWDC 2026 and packed a bunch of major updates into its annual Worldwide Developer Conference keynote. The major announcements include Apple’s next generation of Apple Intelligence and their introduction of Siri AI. We also got details on the new iOS 27, iPadOS 27, and macOS 27 Golden Gate, the next version of its desktop operating system, which brings a global slider to adjust the opacity of Liquid Glass UI effects. Here’s what you missed. #Apple #WWDC #Technology

videos.title: Siri AI, Screen Time, and the rest of WWDC 2026: The Vergecast Livestream
videos.description: Apple's annual developer conference keynote was a strange one this year. The company breezed by its normal slew of operating system upgrades, and talked instead about helping people manage their relationships with their devices, and AI. Lots and lots of AI. On this post-keynote livestream, David Pierce, Hayden Field, and Jake Kastrenakes give their first takes on Siri AI, the Apple Intelligence features coming this fall, Apple's new Screen Time design, and everything else we liked and disliked f

videos.title: Apple’s new photo editing tool #Vergecast
videos.description:
What is a photo? Apple’s new photo editing tool lets you reframe a photo by dragging to move the “camera,” changing the angle of a photo or the framing of its subject.

videos.title: Tim Cook says "it's been an honor"
videos.description: Tim Cook joined Apple in 1998 and quickly earned a reputation as a quiet, calculating supply chain wizard. Cook may not have been a Jobs-level product innovator, but he did figure out how to sell a hell of a lot of iPhones. Under Cook, Apple began targeting different market segments with model variants, experimenting with Plus, Mini, and R versions on its way to the five current models it sells today: the 17, 17E, 17 Pro, 17 Pro Max, and Air. Come this Fall, John Ternus will take over as Apple C
